apply: Students will be able to calculate the displacement, velocity, and acceleration of a particle in SHM.
Students will be able to solve problems related to energy transfer and loss in damped oscillations.
Students will be able to determine the frequency and time period of oscillations using given data.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast different types of oscillations and their characteristics.
Students will be able to evaluate the effects of damping on the motion of an oscillating system.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the energy of an oscillating system and its amplitude.
remember: Students will be able to recall the definition of oscillations and types of oscillations.
Students will be able to list the characteristics of SHM and Damped Oscillations.
Students will be able to identify the factors that affect the time period of oscillations.
understand: Students will be able to explain the causes of damping in oscillations and its types.
Students will be able to describe the concept of resonance and its implications in SHM.
Students will be able to discuss the energy changes in oscillating systems.

ncert_objectives: To describe the characteristics of simple harmonic motion (SHM).
To explain the causes of damping in oscillations and its types.
To discuss the energy changes in oscillating systems.
To describe the concept of resonance and its implications in SHM.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate the displacement, velocity, and acceleration of a particle in SHM.
To solve problems related to energy transfer and loss in damped oscillations.
To determine the frequency and time period of oscillations using given data.
To compare and contrast different types of oscillations and their characteristics.
